turns.content: quick question mate. do you know if the new boiler regulations that are coming in affect gas safe engineers or is it a separate cert

It depends on which specific regulations you're referring to. In recent years there have been changes around energy efficiency standards, hydrogen-ready boilers, and updated installation practices. Generally:

- **Gas Safe registration** covers your right to work on gas appliances, and that stays as it is with your annual renewal.
- **Manufacturer-specific training** is needed for certain new boiler models -- you can't install a brand you're not trained on.
- **Building regulations updates** (like Part L energy efficiency standards) sometimes require additional competency certification.

Are you talking about the changes to installation standards for new boiler models, or something else? What have you been hearing?

yeah its the new installation standards. theres updated regs coming in for the newer models and apparently if you havent done the refresher course you cant sign off on fitting them. which is a pain cos half my work is boiler installs

Right, that sounds like an updated competency requirement for the newer condensing and low-emission boiler models. It's been in the pipeline for a while -- the industry has been moving towards tighter standards around installation quality and energy efficiency.

If half your work is boiler installs, this isn't optional -- it's business-critical. Without the updated cert, you'd have to turn away jobs or subcontract the signoff, which eats into your margin.

Do you know when the requirement kicks in, and have you found a course to get the update done?

yeah ive already booked it in. june 15th, its a one day course at a training centre in Solihull actually. costs about 250 quid plus vat. its annoying cos its a days work lost as well but if I dont do it I literally cant fit the new models so theres no choice really

£250 plus VAT and a day's lost earnings is annoying, but you're thinking about it the right way. It's a cost of staying in business, not an optional expense. If you can't sign off on new installs, that's thousands in lost revenue over a year versus a few hundred upfront.

The good news is:
- **It's tax deductible.** The course fee, travel, even lunch -- your accountant can claim it all as a business expense.
- **One day is manageable.** Some of these courses drag on for a week. A single day and you're back at work Wednesday.
- **June timing is decent.** Summer is quieter for boiler installs anyway, so you're not losing a day during peak season.

Does the course cover all the major manufacturers you work with, or is it a generic standards update?

its the generic standards one that covers the new regs across the board. the manufacturer specific ones I already have for Worcester Bosch and Vaillant which is what I fit 90 percent of the time. this is just the regulatory update that everyone needs

That makes sense. Worcester Bosch and Vaillant are the two biggest in the UK residential market, so you've got the main bases covered with the manufacturer certs. This regulatory update is just the box-ticking exercise to stay compliant with the new standards.

Worth noting -- once you've done it, it might be worth mentioning on your website, van signage, or any advertising you do. "Fully certified for latest boiler installation standards" or similar. Customers are getting savvier about asking for credentials, and it sets you apart from plumbers who haven't bothered to update.

thats a good shout actually. I should update my google listing as well cos thats where most of my work comes from these days. people just google plumber near me and if youve got good reviews you win innit

Google Business Profile is absolutely where it's at for local tradespeople. If you're already ranking well and have good reviews, adding your updated certifications to the profile is easy and free. You can:

- Add it to your business description
- Post an update -- something like "Just completed the latest boiler installation certification. Fully up to date with 2026 regulations."
- Upload a photo of the certificate once you've got it

Google loves activity on business profiles. Even just posting an update every couple of weeks -- a completed job photo, a seasonal tip about boiler maintenance -- keeps your profile active and pushes you up in search results.

How many reviews have you got at the moment?

about 85 last time I checked and most are 5 stars. I always ask customers to leave a review after a job and most of them do. had a bad one last year from someone who thought I charged too much but I responded politely and explained the pricing so that was fine

85 reviews with mostly 5 stars is excellent -- that's a strong local presence. And responding politely to the negative review was the right call. Potential customers actually look at how you handle criticism, and a professional response to a complaint can work in your favour more than another 5-star review.

Keep doing what you're doing. Ask for reviews, post updates, and add the new cert when you get it in June. At 85 reviews you're probably already ahead of most sole traders in Birmingham, but pushing towards 100 is a nice milestone.

Anything else on the course or the business side?

nah thats everything cheers. just wanted to make sure I wasnt missing anything with the new regs. the course is booked the gas safe is up to date and the reviews are coming in so all good. back to work now got a combi swap in Kings Heath this afternoon

Sounds like you've got everything covered. Gas Safe sorted, course booked for June, business ticking along nicely. Good luck with the combi swap this afternoon -- hope it's a straightforward one. Cheers.
should be alright its a straight swap same location same brand. famous last words though innit haha. cheers mate
Ha, "straightforward" and plumbing in the same sentence is always tempting fate. Hope it goes smoothly. See you later.
